title: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan

seo.title: Authentic Kung Pao Chicken Recipe (Sichuan Style)
seo.metaDescription: Master the authentic Kung Pao Chicken recipe with our Sichuan-style guide. Learn to make this spicy, savory Chinese classic at home with step-by-step instructions. Perfect for dinner!
longTailKeywords: how to make Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan
authentic Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken recipe
easy Kung Pao Chicken recipe at home
traditional Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan style
lowCompetitionKeywords: real Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken recipe
Kung Pao Chicken with Sichuan peppercorns
seo.h1: Authentic Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken
How to Make Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan Style
Tips for the Best Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Plate of authentic Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken with peanuts and chili peppers, served with rice.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the bold and spicy flavors of authentic Sichuan Kung Pao Chicken, a classic Chinese dish perfect for a satisfying meal.

description: Kung Pao Chicken is a vibrant and fiery Sichuan dish, renowned for its delightful balance of spicy, savory, sweet, and tangy flavors. Tender chicken pieces are stir-fried with crunchy peanuts, aromatic Sichuan peppercorns, and dried chilies, creating a mouthwatering experience that truly represents authentic Chinese cuisine. This classic recipe promises a culinary journey to the heart of Sichuan.

ingredients.notes: cut into 1-inch pieces, seeds shaken out
white and light green parts, sliced into 1-inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare Chicken and Marinade
instructions.text: Cut chicken thighs into 1-inch cubes. In a medium bowl, combine chicken with 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine, 1.5 tablespoons cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil. Mix well and let marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Whisk Together the Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine 2 tablespoons light soy sauce, 1 teaspoon dark soy sauce, 2 tablespoons Chinkiang black vinegar, 1.5 tablespoons granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 3 tablespoons chicken broth, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil. Whisk until sugar and cornstarch are fully dissolved. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatics and Chilies
instructions.text: Mince garlic and ginger. Slice scallions into 1-inch pieces, separating white and green parts if desired. Cut dried red chilies into 1-inch pieces and shake out most of the seeds for less heat, or leave them in for more spice.

instructions.title: Stir-Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until smoking. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned and mostly cooked through. Remove chicken from the wok and set aside.

instructions.title: Infuse Flavors with Chilies and Peppercorns
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the wok. Reduce heat to medium. Add dried red chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for 30-60 seconds until fragrant and the chilies turn a darker red. Be careful not to burn them.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Peanuts
instructions.text: Immediately add minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the scallions (if separated) to the wok. Stir-fry for another 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the roasted peanuts and stir briefly.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the wok. Give the prepared sauce a quick whisk again (as cornstarch settles) and pour it over the chicken and aromatics. Stir quickly to coat everything evenly. The sauce will thicken rapidly. Once the sauce has thickened and coats the chicken, stir in the green parts of the scallions.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the Kung Pao Chicken to a serving platter and serve hot with steamed white rice.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, do not skip the Sichuan peppercorns; their unique 'mala' numbing sensation is key to Kung Pao Chicken.
If Shaoxing wine is unavailable, dry sherry can be a substitute, but Shaoxing provides a more authentic depth of flavor.
Serve immediately with steamed white rice to best enjoy the fresh, vibrant flavors and textures. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
article: Kung Pao Chicken Sichuan is a beloved Chinese Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
